2604.19502.title: Beyond Rating: A Comprehensive Evaluation and Benchmark for AI Reviews
2604.19502.paper_summary: The rapid adoption of Large Language Models (LLMs) has spurred interest in automated peer review; however, progress is currently stifled by benchmarks that treat reviewing primarily as a rating prediction task. We argue that the utility of a review lies in its textual justification--its arguments, questions, and critique--rather than a scalar score. To address this, we introduce Beyond Rating, a holistic evaluation framework that assesses AI reviewers across five dimensions: Content Faithfulness, Argumentative Alignment, Focus Consistency, Question Constructiveness, and AI-Likelihood. Notably, we propose a Max-Recall strategy to accommodate valid expert disagreement and introduce a curated dataset of paper with high-confidence reviews, rigorously filtered to remove procedural noise. Extensive experiments demonstrate that while traditional n-gram metrics fail to reflect human preferences, our proposed text-centric metrics--particularly the recall of weakness arguments--correlate strongly with rating accuracy. These findings establish that aligning AI critique focus with human experts is a prerequisite for reliable automated scoring, offering a robust standard for future research.
2604.19502.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）的快速採用激發了對自動同行評審的興趣；然而，目前的進展受到將評審主要視為評分預測任務的基準的限制。我們認為，評審的價值在於其文本上的理由——它的論點、問題和批評——而不是一個數值分數。為了解決這個問題，我們介紹了超越評分（Beyond Rating），這是一個全面的評估框架，從五個維度評估人工智慧評審者：內容忠實度、論證一致性、焦點一致性、問題建設性和人工智慧可能性。值得注意的是，我們提出了一種最大回憶（Max-Recall）策略，以容納有效的專家分歧，並引入了一個經過精心策劃的高信心評審論文數據集，嚴格過濾以去除程序噪音。大量實驗表明，雖然傳統的 n-gram 指標未能反映人類偏好，但我們提出的以文本為中心的指標——特別是弱點論點的回憶——與評分準確性有著強烈的相關性。這些發現確立了將人工智慧批評焦點與人類專家對齊是可靠自動評分的前提，為未來的研究提供了一個穩健的標準。
2604.19502.author: Bowen Li et.al.
2604.19502.authors: Bowen Li, Haochen Ma, Yuxin Wang, Jie Yang, Xinchi Chen, Xuanjing Huang, Yining Zheng, Xipeng Qiu
